Comment.value: This translation flow is designed for Title Translations
Script is called from Import text button in Title Localisation Card Window when translation method = "Human"

Stage Name Text status HT Text status MT 1. Language added - (text does not exist) - (text does not exist) 2. Text prepared for translation Not translated Not translate 3. Original text sent Not translated Sent to API + async translation id:¶[id_from_api] (async only) 4. Translated text imported Not reviewed Not reviewed 5. Translation in review In progress In progress 6. Translation approved Approved Approved

Calculation.Text: "You are about to import texts to this localisation. Your current localisation stage are: " & $localisation_stage_name & ". This means that all imported texts will be labelled \"" & $text_status & "\".¶¶ All texts with review status = Approved will not be altered during import. ¶¶ Click OK to move on or Cancel to change the localisation stage before importing texts. "

Comment.value: NOTE! The script step below works ONLY if the field is present on the current layout!!!!

Calculation.Text: "Select a text JSON file to import"

Comment.value: ------------------ Make sure the selected file have the right localisation code -----------------------------------
The text file does NOT have the correct langugage suffix

Calculation.Text: "The selected file does not seem correctly named."
"The file should have a suffix and an extension as: " & $localisation_code & ".json.¶¶ Please select another file."

Comment.value: Just validate the JSON - but do not SEND as a script parameter - FileMaker limits script parameters to 1.000.000 characters.
------------------ Make sure the selected file is a proper JSON -----------------------------------
Content in file is NOT a valid JSON

Calculation.Text: "Sorry but the content in the selected file does not seem to be a valid json object. Please validate the content at https://jsonlint.com/"

Comment.value: Always start with finding texts using the unique id for the field.

WARNING! The pairing mode below is only allowed when we by mistake sent original texts to translation agency WITHOUT having creating localised text fields first. Be very careful since this import can overwrite existing localisations if language setting is not correct.

Calculation.Text: "The imported texts cannot be paired using their unique identifier. ¶¶Do you want to import and pair the texts using id + localisation code? Make sure you have imported the right language (" & $localisation_code & ") before proceeding. Your selected filename is: \"" & $filename & "\"."

Comment.value: Import - pair with primaryKey + localisation_code instead
---------------------- Stage 4 - Change stage on Title and Package localisations --------------------------------------------

Calculation.Text: Case ( $num_of_untranslated_texts=0 And $num_of_translated_texts > 0; "Successfully imported " & $num_of_translated_texts & " texts from "& $translation_provider & ", File: \"" & $filename & "\"."; $num_of_untranslated_texts > 0 And $num_of_translated_texts > 0; "Successfully imported " & $num_of_translated_texts & " texts from " & $translation_provider & ". Could not import " & $num_of_untranslated_texts & " texts."; $num_of_translated_texts = 0; "No texts imported from " & $translation_provider & ". Check JSON-file for errors or missing data." )

Comment.value: Make sure all PSOS changes are viewed in window
